title: Miconia haughtii
intro: Miconia haughtii, also known as the "Pride of Puerto Rico," is a shrub-like plant species native to the tropical forests of Puerto Rico and the Dominican Republic, belonging to the family Melastomataceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, colds, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Miconia haughtii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Miconia haughtii is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into salves and creams for topical use.
taxonomy: Miconia haughtii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. This family, known as the melastome or memecyclid family, consists of approximately 170 genera and over 5,000 species. Miconia haughtii is further classified as a member of the genus Miconia, which comprises over 1,500 species of plants.
